sourceMovie.title: La Haine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: La Haine works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Léon: The Professional
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If La Haine clicked for you, Léon: The Professional (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after La Haine?
faq.answer: Try Léon: The Professional, American History X, 8 Mile—each shares DNA with La Haine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is La Haine?
faq.answer: La Haine (1995) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is La Haine worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
